section_name: Exemption from Inscription Requirement for Certain Municipal and County-owned Motor Vehicles
chapter_title: Inscription Required on State, Mun., & Cnty. Motor Vehicles

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: The governing body of a municipality may exempt from the requirements of Section 721.004 (Inscription Required on Municipal and County-owned Motor Vehicles and Heavy Equipment) :
an automobile when used to perform an official duty by a:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: police department;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: magistrate as defined by Article 2A.151 (Types of Magistrates) , Code of Criminal Procedure;

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: medical examiner;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: municipal code enforcement officer designated to enforce environmental criminal laws; or

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: municipal fire marshal or arson investigator; or
an automobile used by a municipal employee only when conducting an investigation involving suspected fraud or other mismanagement within the municipality.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The commissioners court of a county may exempt from the requirements of Section 721.004 (Inscription Required on Municipal and County-owned Motor Vehicles and Heavy Equipment) :
an automobile when used to perform an official duty by a:

subsections.label: A
subsections.text: police department;

subsections.label: B
subsections.text: sheriff’s office;

subsections.label: C
subsections.text: constable’s office;

subsections.label: D
subsections.text: criminal district attorney’s office;

subsections.label: E
subsections.text: district attorney’s office;

subsections.label: F
subsections.text: county attorney’s office;

subsections.label: G
subsections.text: magistrate as defined by Article 2A.151 (Types of Magistrates) , Code of Criminal Procedure;

subsections.label: H
subsections.text: county fire marshal’s office; or

subsections.label: I
subsections.text: medical examiner; or
a juvenile probation department vehicle used to transport children, when used to perform an official duty.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: An exemption provided under this section does not apply to a contract deputy.

history.raw: Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 765 (H.B.4504), Sec. 2.179, eff. January 1, 2025.
